calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-13-2026 Continued To Jun-23-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-29-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Mar-24-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Apr-16-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. MICHAEL JAHANGANI, Esq. of Quill & Arrow, LLP (tel: 310-933-4271) for Plaintiff.
Atty. KEVIN TULLY, Esq. of Law Office of Kevin J. Tully (tel: 650-377-0708) for Defendant.

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF ALEXANDRA VIVEROS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs Request For Production Of Documents, Set One.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications.

Plaintiff Alexandra Viveros's motion to compel further responses to her request for production of documents, set one, from Ford Motor Company is denied.

The court finds that Viveros did not sufficiently meet and confer before bringing this motion. Viveros sent a meet and confer letter to Ford's counsel that attached two sample discovery orders. On February 12, 2025, Ford offered to produce the documents outlined in those sample orders, subjective to a protective order. (Tully Dec. para. 6 & Ex. 5.) On February 18, Viveros returned the protective order. A few days later, Viveros moved to compel without any further efforts to narrow the dispute, after Ford compromised in response to Viveros's letter.

When a party fails to meet and confer as required by the discovery statutes, the court must choose between the harsh remedy of denying relief altogether and lesser remedies. (See Obregon v. Superior Court (1998) 67 Cal.App.4th 424, 435.) Here, however, Viveros's motion fails to explain why Ford's offer to produce the documents in the sample orders Viveros provided was insufficient. Thus, Viveros's motion fails to carry her burden of setting forth specific facts showing good cause for production. (See CCP 2031.310(b)(1).) The court denies the motion, subject to Ford's agreeing to produce the documents it offered to produce in its February 12, 2025 letter.

Neither party seeks sanctions, but CCP 2031.300(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to a demand for inspection . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The failure to meet and confer is a misuse of the discovery process and burdens the court with reviewing disputes that the parties could resolve through good-faith efforts. (CCP 2023.010(i).) The court finds that Viveros acted without substantial justification in failing to meet and confer and orders her to pay sanctions of $500 to the San Francisco Superior Court within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
